display.purpose: Hero totals and auth headings only — one serif moment per screen.

headline.purpose: Section headers: Recap, Monthly budget, Transactions.

shadows.name: shadow-stroke
shadows.purpose: Default bordered-card look without adding to layout width.

shadows.name: shadow-widget
shadows.purpose: The one primary-action card (Add a receipt) and lifted elements.

shadows.name: shadow-dropdown
shadows.purpose: Floating surfaces: bulk-action toolbar, receipt-viewer modal.

motion.name: ease-out-quint
motion.purpose: Default easing for progress bars, width/opacity transitions.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Default action button — Save, Sign in, Add for this month.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; height: 48px; padding: 0 20px; border-radius: 12px; background: #2A85FF; color: #FFFFFF; font: 700 15px/1.6 Inter, sans-serif; border: none; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 0.2s; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #1B6FDB; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #2A85FF; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-primary:disabled { opacity: 0.5; pointer-events: none; }

components.name: Stroke Button
components.description: Secondary action — Cancel, Clear, Choose another photo.
components.css: .ds-btn-stroke { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; height: 48px; padding: 0 20px; border-radius: 12px; background: transparent; color: #1A1D1F; font: 700 15px/1.6 Inter, sans-serif; border: none; box-shadow: inset 0 0 0 2px #EFEFEF; cursor: pointer; transition: box-shadow 0.2s; } .ds-btn-stroke:hover { box-shadow: inset 0 0 0 2px #1A1D1F; }

components.name: Status Pill
components.description: Closed five-color badge vocabulary — recurring/info(blue), on-track(green), over-budget(red), tag(purple), edited/near-limit(yellow).
components.css: .ds-status { display: inline-block; border-radius: 6px; padding: 0 8px; font: 700 12px/24px Inter, sans-serif; } .ds-status-blue { background: #B1E5FC; color: #2A85FF; } .ds-status-green { background: #EAFAE5; color: #83BF6E; } .ds-status-red { background: #FFE7E4; color: #FF6A55; } .ds-status-purple { background: #CABDFF; color: #8E59FF; } .ds-status-yellow { background: #FFD88D; color: #1A1D1F; }

components.name: Card
components.description: Flat-by-default content container; the app's one universal surface.
components.html:
Monthly budget

Card content goes here.
components.css: .ds-card { background: #FCFCFC; border-radius: 8px; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px #EFEFEF inset; padding: 24px; } .ds-card-title { font: 600 20px/1.6 Inter, sans-serif; letter-spacing: -0.02em; color: #1A1D1F; margin-bottom: 32px; }

components.name: Field
components.description: Text input — flat neutral fill at rest, border + white background on focus.
components.css: .ds-field { height: 48px; width: 100%; border-radius: 12px; border: 2px solid transparent; background: #F4F4F4; padding: 0 10px; font: 600 15px/1.6 Inter, sans-serif; color: #1A1D1F; outline: none; transition: border-color 0.2s, background-color 0.2s; } .ds-field::placeholder { color: #6F767E; } .ds-field:focus { border-color: #9A9FA5; background: #FFFFFF; }

components.name: Checkbox
components.description: 24×24 tick-box with a filled Signal Blue checked state — used for row selection and boolean toggles (e.g. recurring).
components.css: .ds-checkbox { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; cursor: pointer; user-select: none; } .ds-checkbox-input { position: absolute; opacity: 0; } .ds-checkbox-box { position: relative; display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 24px; height: 24px; border-radius: 6px; border: 2px solid rgba(111,118,126,0.4); background: #2A85FF; transition: border-color 0.2s, background-color 0.2s; } .ds-checkbox-tick { width: 17px; height: 12px; } .ds-checkbox-label { padding-left: 16px; font: 600 15px/1.6 Inter, sans-serif; color: #1A1D1F; }

narrative.overview: Notarik removes the data-entry chore that kills the spending-tracking habit. The interface is the opposite of the spreadsheet it replaces: unhurried, legible, and trustworthy. The numbers are the product, so the visual system gets out of their way. A serif display voice (Fraunces) marks the moments that matter — the recap headline, a budget total, a sign-in heading — while a dense, well-tuned sans (Inter) carries every working surface. Restraint is the doctrine: a single indigo accent carries primary actions and selection, never decoration, and a five-color status vocabulary gives every badge and flag a consistent, calm home instead of one-off treatments per feature.
keyCharacteristics: Calm, legible, trust-first — the data leads, the chrome recedes.
Restrained color: tinted neutrals + one indigo accent (≤10% of any screen) + a fixed status-pill vocabulary.
Editorial hierarchy (Fraunces serif) over dense, scannable Inter body.
A shared component kit (components/ui) is the single source of truth for every screen.
Honesty as a design value: estimates look like estimates, corrections are visibly flagged.

rules.name: The One Voice Rule
rules.body: Signal Blue is the only color that ever means "act here" or "this is selected." It appears on ≤10% of any screen.

rules.name: The Closed Status Set Rule
rules.body: Every badge, flag, or state indicator uses one of the five Status variants. Never invent a new inline color treatment for a new feature.

rules.name: The Honest-Money Rule
rules.body: Projection and estimate surfaces never use the primary accent as if they were facts.

rules.name: The Tabular-Figures Rule
rules.body: Every monetary value uses tabular lining figures. Misaligned digits read as untrustworthy in a money tool.

rules.name: The One-Serif-Moment Rule
rules.body: Fraunces appears only on hero figures and page/auth headings — never on a button, label, badge, or table cell.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest; shadow-widget and shadow-dropdown are reserved for the one primary action and genuinely floating elements.

dos: Do pull every button, card, form field, checkbox, toggle, and badge from components/ui — never hand-roll an equivalent with raw Tailwind on a new screen.
Do keep the data the hero — tinted neutrals for surfaces, Signal Blue reserved for action/selection/state.
Do use tabular lining figures (.nums) for every monetary value.
Do make body text ≥4.5:1 against its background; bump toward Ink if contrast is even close.
Do give every state an honest treatment: loading copy, an empty state that teaches the first-receipt flow, clear error recovery.
Do keep projections visibly labelled as estimates and styled with the dashed/muted Projection treatment.
Do vary spacing for rhythm — tight within a related group, generous between groups.
donts: Don't ship the crypto/neon dashboard look: no neon gradients, no glassmorphism, no hype-y data viz.
Don't recreate the spreadsheet it replaces: no gridlines-everywhere, no gray-on-gray, no flat hierarchy.
Don't use a warm cream/sand/paper body background.
Don't use tracked all-caps eyebrows, numbered section markers, or identical icon-card grids.
Don't use gradient text or colored side-stripe borders anywhere except the one deliberate Title bar exception.
Don't let Signal Blue leak into decoration — if it's on more than ~10% of the screen, something is wrong.
Don't invent a one-off badge color for a new feature; map every flag to one of the five closed Status variants.
Don't apply shadow-widget to a passive data card for texture.
